name: Michigan State University Extension - Clare C
description_short: Michigan State University Extension provides free, unbiased financial education and housing counseling through certified advisors, helping residents with money management, homeownership, and foreclosure prevention.
description_long: Michigan State University Extension's MI Money Health program is a university-based financial education initiative operated by MSU Extension, a public outreach arm of Michigan State University. The program is funded through the university system and operates as a free educational resource rather than a commercial service. MI Money Health was established with the mission that every Michigan resident has the opportunity to participate in personal finance and homeownership education, regardless of their financial status or background. The program has been tracking and publishing impact data since at least 2020, demonstrating institutional commitment to outcomes measurement and accountability. Background: MSU Extension is embedded within Michigan State University's land-grant mission to provide accessible education to the public. The MI Money Health program leverages university resources and academic expertise to deliver financial guidance at no cost to residents. Services and Offerings: MI Money Health provides both online and in-person classes covering money management, foreclosure prevention, homeownership education, and retirement planning. They offer self-paced learning modules available through multiple formats including articles, videos, courses, and publications. The program maintains certified housing and money management counselors available for direct consultation. They deliver specialized presentations including the Rent Smart program (financial preparation for renters) and "Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Plate?" (estate planning education). Regular webinars and in-person seminars are scheduled throughout the year on topics like mortgage foreclosure basics and homeownership education. Youth engagement includes programs like Smart Money Missions Goosechase Game, a scavenger hunt targeting ages 13-19. What Distinguishes Them: MSU Extension emphasizes providing "trusted, unbiased education" that is intentionally designed to be easy to understand and demystify complex financial concepts. As a university program, they have no commercial incentive to sell products or services. They serve a deliberately diverse audience and explicitly state their goal to reach residents regardless of financial status. The program is embedded within a large educational institution with substantial research capabilities, which shows through their published impact reports tracking program outcomes. Honest Assessment: This is a legitimate non-profit educational program with no hidden commercial agenda. The free model is genuinely sustainable because it's funded through university appropriations and grants, not by selling consumer products. However, as a primarily educational resource rather than intensive case management service, they may not provide the depth of ongoing one-on-one counseling that someone in active foreclosure or severe financial crisis might need, though they do have counselors available for consultation.
pros: Completely free services with no hidden fees or product sales agenda
Staff includes certified housing and money management counselors
University-backed program with published impact reports and outcome tracking
Multiple learning formats available including webinars, in-person classes, self-paced modules, videos, and articles
Explicit commitment to serving diverse audiences regardless of financial status
Covers full spectrum of financial topics from basic money management to retirement planning and foreclosure prevention
Specializes in reaching underserved populations including youth and renters
cons: Services are Michigan-specific only, not available to out-of-state residents
Limited to educational resources rather than intensive hands-on case management for complex situations
Website navigation is somewhat academic in structure, which may feel less user-friendly than commercial finance sites
Program relies on university funding, which could create sustainability concerns in future budget cycles
best_for: Michigan residents seeking free financial education without sales pressure
Homebuyers preparing for mortgage process who want unbiased guidance
Renters wanting to understand tenant rights and financial preparation
Families facing foreclosure seeking prevention resources and counseling
diagnosis: MI Money Health is best for Michigan residents seeking completely free, unbiased financial education and housing counseling backed by a major university. The main caveat is that while their educational resources are comprehensive and their certified counselors are available, this program is designed as an educational service rather than as intensive case management, making it most effective for people seeking guidance and learning rather than those in acute financial crisis requiring daily crisis intervention.
services: Free financial and housing counseling from certified counselors
Online homeownership education webinars and seminars
Foreclosure prevention assistance and mortgage foreclosure basics education
Money management and personal finance education courses
Self-paced learning modules with articles, videos, and publications
Rent Smart program education for tenants
Estate planning education ("Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?")
Youth financial literacy programs (Smart Money Missions Goosechase Game)
Community presentations on emerging financial issues
One-on-one consultation with financial and housing experts
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
